title: auth — HTTP middleware
description: Five owned middleware. Each enforces an auth-flow-specific invariant BEFORE the endpoint handler runs. Ordering matters — the priorities below are the relative order the framework applies them within the auth-flow middleware group.

middleware.description: Per-IP + per-email rate limit on login / password-reset / MFA verify. 5 attempts per 15 minutes per IP; 10 attempts per hour per email. Uses laravel/framework's RateLimiter with separate buckets per (ip, email). Returns 429 with Retry-After header on exceed. This is the CHEAPEST rejection surface — runs BEFORE any DB call.

Validates the request's X-Application-Id matches the Sanctum PAT's Application scope (from the PAT's abilities compact array). Refuses with 403 `application_mismatch` when a token issued on Sports is presented on Marketplace. Also refuses when the PAT's tenant scope doesn't match the caller's active tenant_member.
consumed_by: every tenant-plane authenticated route in this module + downstream tenant modules

middleware.description: Verifies an inter-service JWT (Authorization: Bearer <jwt>) against the JWKS. Resolves kid → signing key → signature check + `iss` + `aud` + `exp` + `iat` (with auth.jwt.clock_skew_seconds tolerance) + deny-list check (auth_jwt_deny_list). On success populates auth.jwt_claims on the request; on failure returns 401 `jwt_invalid` or 403 `jwt_denied`. Reserved for downstream service-to-service calls — NOT applied on user-facing HTTP routes.

Strict CORS. Whitelist origins per Application via config('auth.cors.applications.<app_slug>'). No wildcard origins. No credentials-with-wildcard. No unlisted headers. On preflight failure returns 403 immediately — the browser fails the request.
consumed_by: every route in this module (tenant + platform + public JWKS)

middleware.description: AFTER authentication resolves, compute the request's device fingerprint (via DeviceFingerprinter) and compare against the User's known fingerprints in the last auth.session.new_device_lookback_days. Mismatch → fire LoginFromNewDeviceDetected event (async) so the notification pipeline can dispatch LoginFromNewDeviceNotification. Runs on the LOGIN endpoint (after login succeeded) — not on subsequent requests.

body.message: Too many login attempts. Try again later.
This session cannot be used with the requested Application.
body.detail: The session was issued for a different Application. Log in again with the correct Application context.

body_invalid.message: The JWT signature is invalid or the token has expired.
